The '''Miy'til assault bomber''' was a [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]] starfighter.

==Characteristics==
Based on the same [[Hapan]] principles of speed and agility that produced the smaller [[Miy'til starfighter]], the Miy'til assault bomber blended an impressive mix of [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speed and maneuverability that put it on par with a heavy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] rather than a [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]].<ref name="EGW" />

Because of its unusual speed and agility for a bomber, it was highly effective in both atmosphere and space, and was far superior to similarly classed bombers, such as the [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]].<ref name="EGW" />

The crew consists of the pilot, a gunner and an astromech droid .<ref name="SotGSaga" />

==History==
It was so rarely employed by the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]] that even as late as [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], its abilities were poorly understood by foreign powers. As such, the ship proved to be a nasty surprise in first time engagements.<ref name="EGW" />

==Behind the scenes==
''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' (page 44) states that [[Nova-class battle cruiser]]s could transport six assault bombers, but the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] had not information about the bomber; the term Miy'til wasn't used in the source.

[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]] notes that the hyperdrive can only jump to 5 coordinates stored by the astromech droid. Other sources don't mention this limit, but don't make clear if the ship has a navcomputer either.
